sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।स्वर्गं त्रिविष्टपं प्राप्य मम पूर्वपितामहाः ।पाण्डवा धार्तराष्ट्राश्च कानि स्थानानि भेजिरे ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,svargaṁ triviṣṭapaṁ prāpya mama pūrvapitāmahāḥ ,pāṇḍavā dhārtarāṣṭrāśca kāni sthānāni bhejire.
english: Janamejaya said: "Having attained the celestial realm, my ancestors, the Pāṇḍavas and the Dhārtarāṣṭras, what places did they come to inhabit?"
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "Having attained the celestial realm, my ancestors, the Pāṇḍavas and the Dhārtarāṣṭras, what places did they come to inhabit?"

sanskrit: एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं सर्वविच्चासि मे मतः ।महर्षिणाभ्यनुज्ञातो व्यासेनाद्भुतकर्मणा ॥२॥
transliteration: etadicchāmyahaṁ śrotuṁ sarvaviccāsi me mataḥ ,maharṣiṇābhyanujñāto vyāsenādbhutakarmaṇā.
english: I wish to hear this, and I consider you to be all-knowing, as you have been instructed by the great sage Vyāsa of wondrous deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. I wish to hear this, and I consider you to be all-knowing, as you have been instructed by the great sage Vyāsa of wondrous deeds.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स्वर्गं त्रिविष्टपं प्राप्य तव पूर्वपितामहाः ।युधिष्ठिरप्रभृतयो यदकुर्वत तच्छृणु ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,svargaṁ triviṣṭapaṁ prāpya tava pūrvapitāmahāḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraprabhṛtayo yadakurvata tacchṛṇu.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Listen to what your ancestors, led by Yudhiṣṭhira and others, did after attaining the celestial realm."
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. Vaiśampāyana said: "Listen to what your ancestors, led by Yudhiṣṭhira and others, did after attaining the celestial realm."

sanskrit: स्वर्गं त्रिविष्टपं प्राप्य धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।दुर्योधनं श्रिया जुष्टं ददर्शासीनमासने ॥४॥
transliteration: svargaṁ triviṣṭapaṁ prāpya dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,duryodhanaṁ śriyā juṣṭaṁ dadarśāsīnamāsane.
english: Having attained the celestial realm, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), saw Duryodhana seated on a throne, endowed with great splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. Having attained the celestial realm, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), saw Duryodhana seated on a throne, endowed with great splendor.

sanskrit: भ्राजमानमिवादित्यं वीरलक्ष्म्याभिसंवृतम् ।देवैर्भ्राजिष्णुभिः साध्यैः सहितं पुण्यकर्मभिः ॥५॥
transliteration: bhrājamānamivādityaṁ vīralakṣmyābhisaṁvṛtam ,devairbhrājiṣṇubhiḥ sādhyaiḥ sahitaṁ puṇyakarmabhiḥ.
english: He was shining like the sun, encompassed by the splendor of heroes, accompanied by resplendent gods and Sādhyas who perform righteous actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. He was shining like the sun, encompassed by the splendor of heroes, accompanied by resplendent gods and Sādhyas who perform righteous actions.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो दृष्ट्वा दुर्योधनममर्षितः ।सहसा संनिवृत्तोऽभूच्छ्रियं दृष्ट्वा सुयोधने ॥६॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro dṛṣṭvā duryodhanamamarṣitaḥ ,sahasā saṁnivṛtto'bhūcchriyaṁ dṛṣṭvā suyodhane.
english: Then Yudhiṣṭhira, seeing Duryodhana, became indignant. He suddenly turned back, having perceived the prosperity (śrī) of Suyodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. Then Yudhiṣṭhira, seeing Duryodhana, became indignant. He suddenly turned back, having perceived the prosperity (śrī) of Suyodhana.

sanskrit: ब्रुवन्नुच्चैर्वचस्तान्वै नाहं दुर्योधनेन वै ।सहितः कामये लोकाँल्लुब्धेनादीर्घदर्शिना ॥७॥
transliteration: bruvannuccairvacastānvai nāhaṁ duryodhanena vai ,sahitaḥ kāmaye lokāँllubdhenādīrghadarśinā.
english: Speaking loudly these words to them: 'Indeed, I do not desire worlds in company with Duryodhana, who is greedy and lacks foresight.'
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Speaking loudly these words to them: 'Indeed, I do not desire worlds in company with Duryodhana, who is greedy and lacks foresight.'

sanskrit: यत्कृते पृथिवी सर्वा सुहृदो बान्धवास्तथा ।हतास्माभिः प्रसह्याजौ क्लिष्टैः पूर्वं महावने ॥८॥
transliteration: yatkṛte pṛthivī sarvā suhṛdo bāndhavāstathā ,hatāsmābhiḥ prasahyājau kliṣṭaiḥ pūrvaṁ mahāvane.
english: On whose account the entire earth's populace, along with friends and relatives, were forcibly slain by us in battle - by us who were previously afflicted in the great forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. On whose account the entire earth's populace, along with friends and relatives, were forcibly slain by us in battle - by us who were previously afflicted in the great forest.

sanskrit: द्रौपदी च सभामध्ये पाञ्चाली धर्मचारिणी ।परिक्लिष्टानवद्याङ्गी पत्नी नो गुरुसंनिधौ ॥९॥
transliteration: draupadī ca sabhāmadhye pāñcālī dharmacāriṇī ,parikliṣṭānavadyāṅgī patnī no gurusaṁnidhau.
english: Draupadi, the princess of Pañcāla, who is devoted to her intrinsic nature (dharma), our wife with flawless limbs, now distressed, is here in the midst of the assembly, in the presence of our revered elders.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. Draupadi, the princess of Pañcāla, who is devoted to her intrinsic nature (dharma), our wife with flawless limbs, now distressed, is here in the midst of the assembly, in the presence of our revered elders.

sanskrit: स्वस्ति देवा न मे कामः सुयोधनमुदीक्षितुम् ।तत्राहं गन्तुमिच्छामि यत्र ते भ्रातरो मम ॥१०॥
transliteration: svasti devā na me kāmaḥ suyodhanamudīkṣitum ,tatrāhaṁ gantumicchāmi yatra te bhrātaro mama.
english: "Hail, O gods! I have no desire to look upon Suyodhana (Duryodhana). I wish to go where those brothers of mine are."
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. "Hail, O gods! I have no desire to look upon Suyodhana (Duryodhana). I wish to go where those brothers of mine are."

sanskrit: मैवमित्यब्रवीत्तं तु नारदः प्रहसन्निव ।स्वर्गे निवासो राजेन्द्र विरुद्धं चापि नश्यति ॥११॥
transliteration: maivamityabravīttaṁ tu nāradaḥ prahasanniva ,svarge nivāso rājendra viruddhaṁ cāpi naśyati.
english: Narada, as if laughing, said to him, "Do not act thus. O king among men, in heaven (svarga), there is residence, and all opposition (viruddham) also perishes."
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. Narada, as if laughing, said to him, "Do not act thus. O king among men, in heaven (svarga), there is residence, and all opposition (viruddham) also perishes."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर महाबाहो मैवं वोचः कथंचन ।दुर्योधनं प्रति नृपं शृणु चेदं वचो मम ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira mahābāho maivaṁ vocaḥ kathaṁcana ,duryodhanaṁ prati nṛpaṁ śṛṇu cedaṁ vaco mama.
english: "O Yudhishthira, O mighty-armed one, do not speak thus at all. And listen to these words of mine concerning King Duryodhana."
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. "O Yudhishthira, O mighty-armed one, do not speak thus at all. And listen to these words of mine concerning King Duryodhana."

sanskrit: एष दुर्योधनो राजा पूज्यते त्रिदशैः सह ।सद्भिश्च राजप्रवरैर्य इमे स्वर्गवासिनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: eṣa duryodhano rājā pūjyate tridaśaiḥ saha ,sadbhiśca rājapravarairya ime svargavāsinaḥ.
english: This King Duryodhana is worshipped by the gods and by the excellent, virtuous kings who reside in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. This King Duryodhana is worshipped by the gods and by the excellent, virtuous kings who reside in heaven.

sanskrit: वीरलोकगतिं प्राप्तो युद्धे हुत्वात्मनस्तनुम् ।यूयं सर्वे सुरसमा येन युद्धे समासिताः ॥१४॥
transliteration: vīralokagatiṁ prāpto yuddhe hutvātmanastanum ,yūyaṁ sarve surasamā yena yuddhe samāsitāḥ.
english: He attained the destination of heroes, having offered his own body in battle. All of you, who are equal to the gods, were confronted by him in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. He attained the destination of heroes, having offered his own body in battle. All of you, who are equal to the gods, were confronted by him in battle.

sanskrit: स एष क्षत्रधर्मेण स्थानमेतदवाप्तवान् ।भये महति योऽभीतो बभूव पृथिवीपतिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa eṣa kṣatradharmeṇa sthānametadavāptavān ,bhaye mahati yo'bhīto babhūva pṛthivīpatiḥ.
english: This very person, the lord of the earth, attained this position by adhering to the intrinsic nature (kṣatra dharma) of a warrior, remaining fearless in great danger.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. This very person, the lord of the earth, attained this position by adhering to the intrinsic nature (kṣatra dharma) of a warrior, remaining fearless in great danger.

sanskrit: न तन्मनसि कर्तव्यं पुत्र यद्द्यूतकारितम् ।द्रौपद्याश्च परिक्लेशं न चिन्तयितुमर्हसि ॥१६॥
transliteration: na tanmanasi kartavyaṁ putra yaddyūtakāritam ,draupadyāśca parikleśaṁ na cintayitumarhasi.
english: My son, you should not keep in your mind that which was caused by gambling. Nor should you dwell on Draupadi's great suffering.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. My son, you should not keep in your mind that which was caused by gambling. Nor should you dwell on Draupadi's great suffering.

sanskrit: ये चान्येऽपि परिक्लेशा युष्माकं द्यूतकारिताः ।संग्रामेष्वथ वान्यत्र न तान्संस्मर्तुमर्हसि ॥१७॥
transliteration: ye cānye'pi parikleśā yuṣmākaṁ dyūtakāritāḥ ,saṁgrāmeṣvatha vānyatra na tānsaṁsmartumarhasi.
english: You should not remember those troubles caused by gambling, nor any other hardships you faced, whether encountered in battles or elsewhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. You should not remember those troubles caused by gambling, nor any other hardships you faced, whether encountered in battles or elsewhere.

sanskrit: समागच्छ यथान्यायं राज्ञा दुर्योधनेन वै ।स्वर्गोऽयं नेह वैराणि भवन्ति मनुजाधिप ॥१८॥
transliteration: samāgaccha yathānyāyaṁ rājñā duryodhanena vai ,svargo'yaṁ neha vairāṇi bhavanti manujādhipa.
english: O lord of men (manujādhipa), unite justly with King Duryodhana. This is heaven; here, enmities do not exist.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. O lord of men (manujādhipa), unite justly with King Duryodhana. This is heaven; here, enmities do not exist.

sanskrit: नारदेनैवमुक्तस्तु कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।भ्रातॄन्पप्रच्छ मेधावी वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ॥१९॥
transliteration: nāradenaivamuktastu kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhrātṝnpapraccha medhāvī vākyametaduvāca ha.
english: Thus addressed by Nārada, the wise (medhāvī) King of Kurus, Yudhiṣṭhira, questioned his brothers and then spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. Thus addressed by Nārada, the wise (medhāvī) King of Kurus, Yudhiṣṭhira, questioned his brothers and then spoke these words.

sanskrit: यदि दुर्योधनस्यैते वीरलोकाः सनातनाः ।अधर्मज्ञस्य पापस्य पृथिवीसुहृदद्रुहः ॥२०॥
transliteration: yadi duryodhanasyaite vīralokāḥ sanātanāḥ ,adharmajñasya pāpasya pṛthivīsuhṛdadruhaḥ.
english: If these eternal abodes of heroes (vīralokāḥ) are for Duryodhana - the one ignorant of (natural) law (dharma), the sinful one, and one who does not harm the friends of the world -
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 20. If these eternal abodes of heroes (vīralokāḥ) are for Duryodhana - the one ignorant of (natural) law (dharma), the sinful one, and one who does not harm the friends of the world -

sanskrit: यत्कृते पृथिवी नष्टा सहया सरथद्विपा ।वयं च मन्युना दग्धा वैरं प्रतिचिकीर्षवः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yatkṛte pṛthivī naṣṭā sahayā sarathadvipā ,vayaṁ ca manyunā dagdhā vairaṁ praticikīrṣavaḥ.
english: Due to whom the earth, along with its horses, chariots, and elephants, was destroyed. And we, consumed by wrath, desire retaliation.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 21. Due to whom the earth, along with its horses, chariots, and elephants, was destroyed. And we, consumed by wrath, desire retaliation.

sanskrit: ये ते वीरा महात्मानो भ्रातरो मे महाव्रताः ।सत्यप्रतिज्ञा लोकस्य शूरा वै सत्यवादिनः ॥२२॥
transliteration: ye te vīrā mahātmāno bhrātaro me mahāvratāḥ ,satyapratijñā lokasya śūrā vai satyavādinaḥ.
english: Those great-souled, heroic brothers of mine, who observed great vows, whose promises to the world were true, and who were indeed brave truth-speakers.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 22. Those great-souled, heroic brothers of mine, who observed great vows, whose promises to the world were true, and who were indeed brave truth-speakers.

sanskrit: तेषामिदानीं के लोका द्रष्टुमिच्छामि तानहम् ।कर्णं चैव महात्मानं कौन्तेयं सत्यसंगरम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: teṣāmidānīṁ ke lokā draṣṭumicchāmi tānaham ,karṇaṁ caiva mahātmānaṁ kaunteyaṁ satyasaṁgaram.
english: Now, what realms do they inhabit? I wish to see them, as well as the great-souled (mahātman) Karna, son of Kunti, who was steadfast in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 23. Now, what realms do they inhabit? I wish to see them, as well as the great-souled (mahātman) Karna, son of Kunti, who was steadfast in battle.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं सात्यकिं च धृष्टद्युम्नस्य चात्मजान् ।ये च शस्त्रैर्वधं प्राप्ताः क्षत्रधर्मेण पार्थिवाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ sātyakiṁ ca dhṛṣṭadyumnasya cātmajān ,ye ca śastrairvadhaṁ prāptāḥ kṣatradharmeṇa pārthivāḥ.
english: I wish to see Dhrishtadyumna, Satyaki, and the sons of Dhrishtadyumna, and those kings who, in accordance with the warrior's intrinsic nature (dharma), met their death by weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 24. I wish to see Dhrishtadyumna, Satyaki, and the sons of Dhrishtadyumna, and those kings who, in accordance with the warrior's intrinsic nature (dharma), met their death by weapons.

sanskrit: क्व नु ते पार्थिवा ब्रह्मन्नैतान्पश्यामि नारद ।विराटद्रुपदौ चैव धृष्टकेतुमुखांश्च तान् ॥२५॥
transliteration: kva nu te pārthivā brahmannaitānpaśyāmi nārada ,virāṭadrupadau caiva dhṛṣṭaketumukhāṁśca tān.
english: O Brahmin Nārada, where are those kings? I do not see Virāṭa and Drupada, nor those others led by Dhṛṣṭaketu.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 25. O Brahmin Nārada, where are those kings? I do not see Virāṭa and Drupada, nor those others led by Dhṛṣṭaketu.

sanskrit: शिखण्डिनं च पाञ्चाल्यं द्रौपदेयांश्च सर्वशः ।अभिमन्युं च दुर्धर्षं द्रष्टुमिच्छामि नारद ॥२६॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍinaṁ ca pāñcālyaṁ draupadeyāṁśca sarvaśaḥ ,abhimanyuṁ ca durdharṣaṁ draṣṭumicchāmi nārada.
english: O Nārada, I wish to see Śikhaṇḍin, the Pāñcāla prince, and all the sons of Draupadī, as well as the formidable Abhimanyu.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 26. O Nārada, I wish to see Śikhaṇḍin, the Pāñcāla prince, and all the sons of Draupadī, as well as the formidable Abhimanyu.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।नेह पश्यामि विबुधा राधेयममितौजसम् ।भ्रातरौ च महात्मानौ युधामन्यूत्तमौजसौ ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,neha paśyāmi vibudhā rādheyamamitaujasam ,bhrātarau ca mahātmānau yudhāmanyūttamaujasau.
english: Yudhishthira said: O gods, I do not see the son of Rādhā (Karṇa) here, who possessed immeasurable valor. Nor do I see the two magnanimous brothers, Yudhāmanyu and Uttamaujas.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Yudhishthira said: O gods, I do not see the son of Rādhā (Karṇa) here, who possessed immeasurable valor. Nor do I see the two magnanimous brothers, Yudhāmanyu and Uttamaujas.

sanskrit: जुहुवुर्ये शरीराणि रणवह्नौ महारथाः ।राजानो राजपुत्राश्च ये मदर्थे हता रणे ॥२॥
transliteration: juhuvurye śarīrāṇi raṇavahnau mahārathāḥ ,rājāno rājaputrāśca ye madarthe hatā raṇe.
english: Where are those great warriors who offered their bodies into the fire of battle? And where are the kings and princes who were slain in combat for my sake (karma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. Where are those great warriors who offered their bodies into the fire of battle? And where are the kings and princes who were slain in combat for my sake (karma)?

sanskrit: क्व ते महारथाः सर्वे शार्दूलसमविक्रमाः ।तैरप्ययं जितो लोकः कच्चित्पुरुषसत्तमैः ॥३॥
transliteration: kva te mahārathāḥ sarve śārdūlasamavikramāḥ ,tairapyayaṁ jito lokaḥ kaccitpuruṣasattamaiḥ.
english: Where are all those great chariot-warriors whose might was like that of a tiger? I hope this realm (loka) has been attained by them, those best among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. Where are all those great chariot-warriors whose might was like that of a tiger? I hope this realm (loka) has been attained by them, those best among men.

sanskrit: यदि लोकानिमान्प्राप्तास्ते च सर्वे महारथाः ।स्थितं वित्त हि मां देवाः सहितं तैर्महात्मभिः ॥४॥
transliteration: yadi lokānimānprāptāste ca sarve mahārathāḥ ,sthitaṁ vitta hi māṁ devāḥ sahitaṁ tairmahātmabhiḥ.
english: O gods, if all those great chariot-warriors have indeed reached these realms (loka), then know me to be residing with those great-souled ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. O gods, if all those great chariot-warriors have indeed reached these realms (loka), then know me to be residing with those great-souled ones.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न तैरवाप्तोऽयं नृपैर्लोकोऽक्षयः शुभः ।न तैरहं विना वत्स्ये ज्ञातिभिर्भ्रातृभिस्तथा ॥५॥
transliteration: kaccinna tairavāpto'yaṁ nṛpairloko'kṣayaḥ śubhaḥ ,na tairahaṁ vinā vatsye jñātibhirbhrātṛbhistathā.
english: I trust that this imperishable, auspicious realm (loka) has been attained by those kings (nṛpa). I shall not dwell without them, my kinsmen, and also my brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. I trust that this imperishable, auspicious realm (loka) has been attained by those kings (nṛpa). I shall not dwell without them, my kinsmen, and also my brothers.

sanskrit: मातुर्हि वचनं श्रुत्वा तदा सलिलकर्मणि ।कर्णस्य क्रियतां तोयमिति तप्यामि तेन वै ॥६॥
transliteration: māturhi vacanaṁ śrutvā tadā salilakarmaṇi ,karṇasya kriyatāṁ toyamiti tapyāmi tena vai.
english: Indeed, having heard my mother's words then, during the water ritual (salilakarma), 'Let the water offering be made for Karṇa' - because of that, I lament.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. Indeed, having heard my mother's words then, during the water ritual (salilakarma), 'Let the water offering be made for Karṇa' - because of that, I lament.

sanskrit: इदं च परितप्यामि पुनः पुनरहं सुराः ।यन्मातुः सदृशौ पादौ तस्याहममितौजसः ॥७॥
transliteration: idaṁ ca paritapyāmi punaḥ punarahaṁ surāḥ ,yanmātuḥ sadṛśau pādau tasyāhamamitaujasaḥ.
english: O gods, I repeatedly grieve over this: that I failed to recognize him, the one of immense strength, whose feet resembled those of my mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. O gods, I repeatedly grieve over this: that I failed to recognize him, the one of immense strength, whose feet resembled those of my mother.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वैव तं नानुगतः कर्णं परबलार्दनम् ।न ह्यस्मान्कर्णसहिताञ्जयेच्छक्रोऽपि संयुगे ॥८॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvaiva taṁ nānugataḥ karṇaṁ parabalārdanam ,na hyasmānkarṇasahitāñjayecchakro'pi saṁyuge.
english: Having seen Karna, the destroyer of enemy forces, I did not follow him. Indeed, not even Indra himself could have conquered us in battle if he had been with us.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. Having seen Karna, the destroyer of enemy forces, I did not follow him. Indeed, not even Indra himself could have conquered us in battle if he had been with us.

sanskrit: तमहं यत्रतत्रस्थं द्रष्टुमिच्छामि सूर्यजम् ।अविज्ञातो मया योऽसौ घातितः सव्यसाचिना ॥९॥
transliteration: tamahaṁ yatratatrasthaṁ draṣṭumicchāmi sūryajam ,avijñāto mayā yo'sau ghātitaḥ savyasācinā.
english: I wish to see the son of Sūrya (Karna) wherever he may be, for he was unknown to me and was killed by Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. I wish to see the son of Sūrya (Karna) wherever he may be, for he was unknown to me and was killed by Arjuna.

sanskrit: भीमं च भीमविक्रान्तं प्राणेभ्योऽपि प्रियं मम ।अर्जुनं चेन्द्रसंकाशं यमौ तौ च यमोपमौ ॥१०॥
transliteration: bhīmaṁ ca bhīmavikrāntaṁ prāṇebhyo'pi priyaṁ mama ,arjunaṁ cendrasaṁkāśaṁ yamau tau ca yamopamau.
english: And Bhīma, of terrible valor, who is dearer to me even than life; and Arjuna, who resembles Indra; and those two twins, who are like the two Yamas.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. And Bhīma, of terrible valor, who is dearer to me even than life; and Arjuna, who resembles Indra; and those two twins, who are like the two Yamas.

sanskrit: द्रष्टुमिच्छामि तां चाहं पाञ्चालीं धर्मचारिणीम् ।न चेह स्थातुमिच्छामि सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि वः ॥११॥
transliteration: draṣṭumicchāmi tāṁ cāhaṁ pāñcālīṁ dharmacāriṇīm ,na ceha sthātumicchāmi satyametadbravīmi vaḥ.
english: I wish to see that Pañcālī, who always adheres to righteousness (dharma). I truly do not wish to remain here; this truth I tell you.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. I wish to see that Pañcālī, who always adheres to righteousness (dharma). I truly do not wish to remain here; this truth I tell you.

sanskrit: किं मे भ्रातृविहीनस्य स्वर्गेण सुरसत्तमाः ।यत्र ते स मम स्वर्गो नायं स्वर्गो मतो मम ॥१२॥
transliteration: kiṁ me bhrātṛvihīnasya svargeṇa surasattamāḥ ,yatra te sa mama svargo nāyaṁ svargo mato mama.
english: What use is heaven to me, deprived of my brothers, O best among the gods? Where they reside, that is my heaven; this [place] is not considered heaven by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. What use is heaven to me, deprived of my brothers, O best among the gods? Where they reside, that is my heaven; this [place] is not considered heaven by me.

sanskrit: देवा ऊचुः ।यदि वै तत्र ते श्रद्धा गम्यतां पुत्र माचिरम् ।प्रिये हि तव वर्तामो देवराजस्य शासनात् ॥१३॥
transliteration: devā ūcuḥ ,yadi vai tatra te śraddhā gamyatāṁ putra māciram ,priye hi tava vartāmo devarājasya śāsanāt.
english: The gods said: 'If you indeed have belief (śraddhā) there, then go, son, without delay. For we act in accordance with the command of the king of gods, in what is dear to you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. The gods said: 'If you indeed have belief (śraddhā) there, then go, son, without delay. For we act in accordance with the command of the king of gods, in what is dear to you.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा तं ततो देवा देवदूतमुपादिशन् ।युधिष्ठिरस्य सुहृदो दर्शयेति परंतप ॥१४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā taṁ tato devā devadūtamupādiśan ,yudhiṣṭhirasya suhṛdo darśayeti paraṁtapa.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having thus spoken to him, the gods then instructed a divine messenger, saying, "Show Yudhiṣṭhira's friends, O tormentor of foes."'
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having thus spoken to him, the gods then instructed a divine messenger, saying, "Show Yudhiṣṭhira's friends, O tormentor of foes."'

sanskrit: ततः कुन्तीसुतो राजा देवदूतश्च जग्मतुः ।सहितौ राजशार्दूल यत्र ते पुरुषर्षभाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kuntīsuto rājā devadūtaśca jagmatuḥ ,sahitau rājaśārdūla yatra te puruṣarṣabhāḥ.
english: Then, King Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, and the divine messenger proceeded together, O tiger among kings, to where those foremost among men (puruṣa) were.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. Then, King Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, and the divine messenger proceeded together, O tiger among kings, to where those foremost among men (puruṣa) were.

sanskrit: अग्रतो देवदूतस्तु ययौ राजा च पृष्ठतः ।पन्थानमशुभं दुर्गं सेवितं पापकर्मभिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: agrato devadūtastu yayau rājā ca pṛṣṭhataḥ ,panthānamaśubhaṁ durgaṁ sevitaṁ pāpakarmabhiḥ.
english: The divine messenger, however, went ahead, while the king followed behind, traversing an inauspicious and difficult path, frequented by those who committed sinful acts (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. The divine messenger, however, went ahead, while the king followed behind, traversing an inauspicious and difficult path, frequented by those who committed sinful acts (karma).

sanskrit: तमसा संवृतं घोरं केशशैवलशाद्वलम् ।युक्तं पापकृतां गन्धैर्मांसशोणितकर्दमम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tamasā saṁvṛtaṁ ghoraṁ keśaśaivalaśādvalam ,yuktaṁ pāpakṛtāṁ gandhairmāṁsaśoṇitakardamam.
english: It was shrouded in ghastly darkness, with human hair and moss forming its turf. It was filled with the stench of evildoers and consisted of mud made of flesh and blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. It was shrouded in ghastly darkness, with human hair and moss forming its turf. It was filled with the stench of evildoers and consisted of mud made of flesh and blood.

sanskrit: दंशोत्थानं सझिल्लीकं मक्षिकामशकावृतम् ।इतश्चेतश्च कुणपैः समन्तात्परिवारितम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: daṁśotthānaṁ sajhillīkaṁ makṣikāmaśakāvṛtam ,itaścetaśca kuṇapaiḥ samantātparivāritam.
english: It was characterized by swarms of gnats and crickets, covered with flies and mosquitoes, and surrounded on all sides by corpses strewn here and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. It was characterized by swarms of gnats and crickets, covered with flies and mosquitoes, and surrounded on all sides by corpses strewn here and there.

sanskrit: अस्थिकेशसमाकीर्णं कृमिकीटसमाकुलम् ।ज्वलनेन प्रदीप्तेन समन्तात्परिवेष्टितम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: asthikeśasamākīrṇaṁ kṛmikīṭasamākulam ,jvalanena pradīptena samantātpariveṣṭitam.
english: It was covered with bones and hair, infested with worms and insects, and completely surrounded by blazing fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. It was covered with bones and hair, infested with worms and insects, and completely surrounded by blazing fire.

sanskrit: अयोमुखैश्च काकोलैर्गृध्रैश्च समभिद्रुतम् ।सूचीमुखैस्तथा प्रेतैर्विन्ध्यशैलोपमैर्वृतम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: ayomukhaiśca kākolairgṛdhraiśca samabhidrutam ,sūcīmukhaistathā pretairvindhyaśailopamairvṛtam.
english: It was assaulted by iron-beaked kākola birds and vultures, and surrounded by needle-mouthed disembodied spirits (preta) resembling the Vindhya mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. It was assaulted by iron-beaked kākola birds and vultures, and surrounded by needle-mouthed disembodied spirits (preta) resembling the Vindhya mountains.

sanskrit: मेदोरुधिरयुक्तैश्च छिन्नबाहूरुपाणिभिः ।निकृत्तोदरपादैश्च तत्र तत्र प्रवेरितैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: medorudhirayuktaiśca chinnabāhūrupāṇibhiḥ ,nikṛttodarapādaiśca tatra tatra praveritaiḥ.
english: And by (entities) endowed with fat and blood, with severed arms, thighs, and hands; and with bellies and feet cut off, scattered here and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 21. And by (entities) endowed with fat and blood, with severed arms, thighs, and hands; and with bellies and feet cut off, scattered here and there.

sanskrit: स तत्कुणपदुर्गन्धमशिवं रोमहर्षणम् ।जगाम राजा धर्मात्मा मध्ये बहु विचिन्तयन् ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa tatkuṇapadurgandhamaśivaṁ romaharṣaṇam ,jagāma rājā dharmātmā madhye bahu vicintayan.
english: That king, whose spirit was righteous (dharma), approached that inauspicious, hair-raising place, which was foul-smelling from corpses, pondering many things in its midst.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 22. That king, whose spirit was righteous (dharma), approached that inauspicious, hair-raising place, which was foul-smelling from corpses, pondering many things in its midst.

sanskrit: ददर्शोष्णोदकैः पूर्णां नदीं चापि सुदुर्गमाम् ।असिपत्रवनं चैव निशितक्षुरसंवृतम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: dadarśoṣṇodakaiḥ pūrṇāṁ nadīṁ cāpi sudurgamām ,asipatravanaṁ caiva niśitakṣurasaṁvṛtam.
english: He saw a river filled with hot water, exceedingly difficult to traverse, and indeed, a forest of sword-leaves, covered with sharp razors.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 23. He saw a river filled with hot water, exceedingly difficult to traverse, and indeed, a forest of sword-leaves, covered with sharp razors.

sanskrit: करम्भवालुकास्तप्ता आयसीश्च शिलाः पृथक् ।लोहकुम्भीश्च तैलस्य क्वाथ्यमानाः समन्ततः ॥२४॥
transliteration: karambhavālukāstaptā āyasīśca śilāḥ pṛthak ,lohakumbhīśca tailasya kvāthyamānāḥ samantataḥ.
english: He also saw scorching sands mixed with parched grain flour, and separate iron stones, as well as iron pots of oil boiling all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 24. He also saw scorching sands mixed with parched grain flour, and separate iron stones, as well as iron pots of oil boiling all around.

sanskrit: कूटशाल्मलिकं चापि दुस्पर्शं तीक्ष्णकण्टकम् ।ददर्श चापि कौन्तेयो यातनाः पापकर्मिणाम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: kūṭaśālmalikaṁ cāpi dusparśaṁ tīkṣṇakaṇṭakam ,dadarśa cāpi kaunteyo yātanāḥ pāpakarmiṇām.
english: He also saw the Kūṭaśālmalī (silk-cotton) tree, agonizing to touch and characterized by sharp thorns. Moreover, Kunti's son (Yudhishthira) witnessed the tortures inflicted upon those who committed wicked deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 25. He also saw the Kūṭaśālmalī (silk-cotton) tree, agonizing to touch and characterized by sharp thorns. Moreover, Kunti's son (Yudhishthira) witnessed the tortures inflicted upon those who committed wicked deeds.

sanskrit: स तं दुर्गन्धमालक्ष्य देवदूतमुवाच ह ।कियदध्वानमस्माभिर्गन्तव्यमिदमीदृशम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa taṁ durgandhamālakṣya devadūtamuvāca ha ,kiyadadhvānamasmābhirgantavyamidamīdṛśam.
english: Perceiving that foul stench, he (Yudhishthira) then spoke to the divine messenger, 'How much more of this kind of path must be traversed by us?'
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 26. Perceiving that foul stench, he (Yudhishthira) then spoke to the divine messenger, 'How much more of this kind of path must be traversed by us?'

sanskrit: क्व च ते भ्रातरो मह्यं तन्ममाख्यातुमर्हसि ।देशोऽयं कश्च देवानामेतदिच्छामि वेदितुम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: kva ca te bhrātaro mahyaṁ tanmamākhyātumarhasi ,deśo'yaṁ kaśca devānāmetadicchāmi veditum.
english: And where are your brothers? You should tell me that. Also, what kind of region of the gods is this? I wish to know this.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 27. And where are your brothers? You should tell me that. Also, what kind of region of the gods is this? I wish to know this.

sanskrit: स संनिववृते श्रुत्वा धर्मराजस्य भाषितम् ।देवदूतोऽब्रवीच्चैनमेतावद्गमनं तव ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa saṁnivavṛte śrutvā dharmarājasya bhāṣitam ,devadūto'bravīccainametāvadgamanaṁ tava.
english: Having heard the words of the King of (dharma), he turned back. The divine messenger then said to him, 'This is the extent of your journey.'
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 28. Having heard the words of the King of (dharma), he turned back. The divine messenger then said to him, 'This is the extent of your journey.'

sanskrit: निवर्तितव्यं हि मया तथास्म्युक्तो दिवौकसैः ।यदि श्रान्तोऽसि राजेन्द्र त्वमथागन्तुमर्हसि ॥२९॥
transliteration: nivartitavyaṁ hi mayā tathāsmyukto divaukasaiḥ ,yadi śrānto'si rājendra tvamathāgantumarhasi.
english: Indeed, I must turn back, for I have been instructed thus by the celestials. If you are weary, O King of kings, then you should come (back).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 29. Indeed, I must turn back, for I have been instructed thus by the celestials. If you are weary, O King of kings, then you should come (back).

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु निर्विण्णस्तेन गन्धेन मूर्छितः ।निवर्तने धृतमनाः पर्यावर्तत भारत ॥३०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu nirviṇṇastena gandhena mūrchitaḥ ,nivartane dhṛtamanāḥ paryāvartata bhārata.
english: However, Yudhiṣṭhira, despondent and faint from that odor, having made up his mind to return, turned back, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 30. However, Yudhiṣṭhira, despondent and faint from that odor, having made up his mind to return, turned back, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: स संनिवृत्तो धर्मात्मा दुःखशोकसमन्वितः ।शुश्राव तत्र वदतां दीना वाचः समन्ततः ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa saṁnivṛtto dharmātmā duḥkhaśokasamanvitaḥ ,śuśrāva tatra vadatāṁ dīnā vācaḥ samantataḥ.
english: He, the one whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, returned, accompanied by sorrow and grief. There, he heard lamenting voices speaking from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 31. He, the one whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, returned, accompanied by sorrow and grief. There, he heard lamenting voices speaking from all directions.

sanskrit: भो भो धर्मज राजर्षे पुण्याभिजन पाण्डव ।अनुग्रहार्थमस्माकं तिष्ठ तावन्मुहूर्तकम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: bho bho dharmaja rājarṣe puṇyābhijana pāṇḍava ,anugrahārthamasmākaṁ tiṣṭha tāvanmuhūrtakam.
english: O, O, son of Dharma (dharma), royal sage, Pāṇḍava of sacred lineage! For the sake of favoring us, please stay for a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 32. O, O, son of Dharma (dharma), royal sage, Pāṇḍava of sacred lineage! For the sake of favoring us, please stay for a moment.

sanskrit: आयाति त्वयि दुर्धर्षे वाति पुण्यः समीरणः ।तव गन्धानुगस्तात येनास्मान्सुखमागमत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: āyāti tvayi durdharṣe vāti puṇyaḥ samīraṇaḥ ,tava gandhānugastāta yenāsmānsukhamāgamat.
english: O dear one, as you, the formidable one, approach, a purifying wind, following your fragrance, blows. Through this, comfort has come to us.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 33. O dear one, as you, the formidable one, approach, a purifying wind, following your fragrance, blows. Through this, comfort has come to us.

sanskrit: ते वयं पार्थ दीर्घस्य कालस्य पुरुषर्षभ ।सुखमासादयिष्यामस्त्वां दृष्ट्वा राजसत्तम ॥३४॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ pārtha dīrghasya kālasya puruṣarṣabha ,sukhamāsādayiṣyāmastvāṁ dṛṣṭvā rājasattama.
english: O Pārtha, O best among persons (puruṣa), O best among kings, having seen you, we shall achieve comfort after a long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 34. O Pārtha, O best among persons (puruṣa), O best among kings, having seen you, we shall achieve comfort after a long time.

sanskrit: संतिष्ठस्व महाबाहो मुहूर्तमपि भारत ।त्वयि तिष्ठति कौरव्य यातनास्मान्न बाधते ॥३५॥
transliteration: saṁtiṣṭhasva mahābāho muhūrtamapi bhārata ,tvayi tiṣṭhati kauravya yātanāsmānna bādhate.
english: O mighty-armed one, O descendant of Bharata, O scion of Kuru, please remain for just a moment. As long as you stay, this torment will not afflict us.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 35. O mighty-armed one, O descendant of Bharata, O scion of Kuru, please remain for just a moment. As long as you stay, this torment will not afflict us.

sanskrit: एवं बहुविधा वाचः कृपणा वेदनावताम् ।तस्मिन्देशे स शुश्राव समन्ताद्वदतां नृप ॥३६॥
transliteration: evaṁ bahuvidhā vācaḥ kṛpaṇā vedanāvatām ,tasmindeśe sa śuśrāva samantādvadatāṁ nṛpa.
english: O king, in that place, he heard from all sides such various pitiable words from those who were suffering and speaking.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 36. O king, in that place, he heard from all sides such various pitiable words from those who were suffering and speaking.

sanskrit: तेषां तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा दयावान्दीनभाषिणाम् ।अहो कृच्छ्रमिति प्राह तस्थौ स च युधिष्ठिरः ॥३७॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tadvacanaṁ śrutvā dayāvāndīnabhāṣiṇām ,aho kṛcchramiti prāha tasthau sa ca yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Having heard those words of theirs, of those who spoke miserably, the compassionate Yudhiṣṭhira exclaimed, 'Oh, what distress!' and he remained there.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 37. Having heard those words of theirs, of those who spoke miserably, the compassionate Yudhiṣṭhira exclaimed, 'Oh, what distress!' and he remained there.

sanskrit: स ता गिरः पुरस्ताद्वै श्रुतपूर्वाः पुनः पुनः ।ग्लानानां दुःखितानां च नाभ्यजानत पाण्डवः ॥३८॥
transliteration: sa tā giraḥ purastādvai śrutapūrvāḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,glānānāṁ duḥkhitānāṁ ca nābhyajānata pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: The son of Pāṇḍu did not recognize those words, which he had certainly heard repeatedly before, from those who were exhausted and suffering.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 38. The son of Pāṇḍu did not recognize those words, which he had certainly heard repeatedly before, from those who were exhausted and suffering.

sanskrit: अबुध्यमानस्ता वाचो धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।उवाच के भवन्तो वै किमर्थमिह तिष्ठथ ॥३९॥
transliteration: abudhyamānastā vāco dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,uvāca ke bhavanto vai kimarthamiha tiṣṭhatha.
english: Uncomprehending those voices, Yudhishthira, the son of (dharma), asked, "Who are you all, and for what purpose are you standing here?"
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 39. Uncomprehending those voices, Yudhishthira, the son of (dharma), asked, "Who are you all, and for what purpose are you standing here?"

sanskrit: इत्युक्तास्ते ततः सर्वे समन्तादवभाषिरे ।कर्णोऽहं भीमसेनोऽहमर्जुनोऽहमिति प्रभो ॥४०॥
transliteration: ityuktāste tataḥ sarve samantādavabhāṣire ,karṇo'haṁ bhīmaseno'hamarjuno'hamiti prabho.
english: When thus addressed, all of them then spoke from all sides, saying, "I am Karna," "I am Bhimasena," "I am Arjuna," O Lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 40. When thus addressed, all of them then spoke from all sides, saying, "I am Karna," "I am Bhimasena," "I am Arjuna," O Lord.

sanskrit: नकुलः सहदेवोऽहं धृष्टद्युम्नोऽहमित्युत ।द्रौपदी द्रौपदेयाश्च इत्येवं ते विचुक्रुशुः ॥४१॥
transliteration: nakulaḥ sahadevo'haṁ dhṛṣṭadyumno'hamityuta ,draupadī draupadeyāśca ityevaṁ te vicukruśuḥ.
english: "I am Nakula," "I am Sahadeva," "I am Dhrishtadyumna!" And moreover, Draupadi and the sons of Draupadi - in this manner, they all cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 41. "I am Nakula," "I am Sahadeva," "I am Dhrishtadyumna!" And moreover, Draupadi and the sons of Draupadi - in this manner, they all cried out.

sanskrit: ता वाचः स तदा श्रुत्वा तद्देशसदृशीर्नृप ।ततो विममृशे राजा किं न्विदं दैवकारितम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tā vācaḥ sa tadā śrutvā taddeśasadṛśīrnṛpa ,tato vimamṛśe rājā kiṁ nvidaṁ daivakāritam.
english: O King, having then heard those voices, which were appropriate to that place, the king then pondered, "What indeed is this, brought about by divine will?"
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 42. O King, having then heard those voices, which were appropriate to that place, the king then pondered, "What indeed is this, brought about by divine will?"

sanskrit: किं नु तत्कलुषं कर्म कृतमेभिर्महात्मभिः ।कर्णेन द्रौपदेयैर्वा पाञ्चाल्या वा सुमध्यया ॥४३॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu tatkaluṣaṁ karma kṛtamebhirmahātmabhiḥ ,karṇena draupadeyairvā pāñcālyā vā sumadhyayā.
english: What sinful act (karma), then, was committed by these great souls - by Karṇa, or by the sons of Draupadī, or by the slender-waisted Pāñcālī (Draupadī)?
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 43. What sinful act (karma), then, was committed by these great souls - by Karṇa, or by the sons of Draupadī, or by the slender-waisted Pāñcālī (Draupadī)?

sanskrit: य इमे पापगन्धेऽस्मिन्देशे सन्ति सुदारुणे ।न हि जानामि सर्वेषां दुष्कृतं पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: ya ime pāpagandhe'smindeśe santi sudāruṇe ,na hi jānāmi sarveṣāṁ duṣkṛtaṁ puṇyakarmaṇām.
english: What is this extremely cruel land, permeated by sin, where these people are present? Indeed, I do not know the evil deed (karma) of all those whose actions are meritorious.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 44. What is this extremely cruel land, permeated by sin, where these people are present? Indeed, I do not know the evil deed (karma) of all those whose actions are meritorious.

sanskrit: किं कृत्वा धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रो राजा सुयोधनः ।तथा श्रिया युतः पापः सह सर्वैः पदानुगैः ॥४५॥
transliteration: kiṁ kṛtvā dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putro rājā suyodhanaḥ ,tathā śriyā yutaḥ pāpaḥ saha sarvaiḥ padānugaiḥ.
english: Having done what (karma), is Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, King Suyodhana, that wicked man, thus endowed with prosperity along with all his followers?
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 45. Having done what (karma), is Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, King Suyodhana, that wicked man, thus endowed with prosperity along with all his followers?

sanskrit: महेन्द्र इव लक्ष्मीवानास्ते परमपूजितः ।कस्येदानीं विकारोऽयं यदिमे नरकं गताः ॥४६॥
transliteration: mahendra iva lakṣmīvānāste paramapūjitaḥ ,kasyedānīṁ vikāro'yaṁ yadime narakaṁ gatāḥ.
english: He (Suyodhana) remains prosperous and highly honored, just like Mahendra. But whose misfortune (karma) is this now, if these others have gone to hell?
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 46. He (Suyodhana) remains prosperous and highly honored, just like Mahendra. But whose misfortune (karma) is this now, if these others have gone to hell?

sanskrit: सर्वधर्मविदः शूराः सत्यागमपरायणाः ।क्षात्रधर्मपराः प्राज्ञा यज्वानो भूरिदक्षिणाः ॥४७॥
transliteration: sarvadharmavidaḥ śūrāḥ satyāgamaparāyaṇāḥ ,kṣātradharmaparāḥ prājñā yajvāno bhūridakṣiṇāḥ.
english: They are knowers of all aspects of natural law (dharma), brave, devoted to truth and sacred texts, dedicated to the warrior's constitution (dharma), wise, performers of Vedic rituals (yajña), and givers of abundant donations.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 47. They are knowers of all aspects of natural law (dharma), brave, devoted to truth and sacred texts, dedicated to the warrior's constitution (dharma), wise, performers of Vedic rituals (yajña), and givers of abundant donations.

sanskrit: किं नु सुप्तोऽस्मि जागर्मि चेतयानो न चेतये ।अहो चित्तविकारोऽयं स्याद्वा मे चित्तविभ्रमः ॥४८॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu supto'smi jāgarmi cetayāno na cetaye ,aho cittavikāro'yaṁ syādvā me cittavibhramaḥ.
english: Am I asleep or am I awake? Though conscious, I do not understand. Oh, is this a mental disturbance, or perhaps a delusion of my mind (citta)?
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 48. Am I asleep or am I awake? Though conscious, I do not understand. Oh, is this a mental disturbance, or perhaps a delusion of my mind (citta)?

sanskrit: एवं बहुविधं राजा विममर्श युधिष्ठिरः ।दुःखशोकसमाविष्टश्चिन्ताव्याकुलितेन्द्रियः ॥४९॥
transliteration: evaṁ bahuvidhaṁ rājā vimamarśa yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,duḥkhaśokasamāviṣṭaścintāvyākulitendriyaḥ.
english: Thus, King Yudhishthira pondered deeply in many ways, overcome by sorrow and grief, his senses agitated by worry.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 49. Thus, King Yudhishthira pondered deeply in many ways, overcome by sorrow and grief, his senses agitated by worry.

sanskrit: क्रोधमाहारयच्चैव तीव्रं धर्मसुतो नृपः ।देवांश्च गर्हयामास धर्मं चैव युधिष्ठिरः ॥५०॥
transliteration: krodhamāhārayaccaiva tīvraṁ dharmasuto nṛpaḥ ,devāṁśca garhayāmāsa dharmaṁ caiva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: And the king, son of Dharma (dharma), Yudhishthira, also manifested intense anger. He blamed both the gods and (natural) law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 50. And the king, son of Dharma (dharma), Yudhishthira, also manifested intense anger. He blamed both the gods and (natural) law (dharma).

sanskrit: स तीव्रगन्धसंतप्तो देवदूतमुवाच ह ।गम्यतां भद्र येषां त्वं दूतस्तेषामुपान्तिकम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: sa tīvragandhasaṁtapto devadūtamuvāca ha ,gamyatāṁ bhadra yeṣāṁ tvaṁ dūtasteṣāmupāntikam.
english: Tormented by the pungent odor, he spoke to the divine messenger: "Go, O good one, to those whose messenger you are, go near them."
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 51. Tormented by the pungent odor, he spoke to the divine messenger: "Go, O good one, to those whose messenger you are, go near them."

sanskrit: न ह्यहं तत्र यास्यामि स्थितोऽस्मीति निवेद्यताम् ।मत्संश्रयादिमे दूत सुखिनो भ्रातरो हि मे ॥५२॥
transliteration: na hyahaṁ tatra yāsyāmi sthito'smīti nivedyatām ,matsaṁśrayādime dūta sukhino bhrātaro hi me.
english: "Indeed, I will not go there; let it be reported that I am staying here. O messenger, these brothers of mine are truly happy because they depend on me."
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 52. "Indeed, I will not go there; let it be reported that I am staying here. O messenger, these brothers of mine are truly happy because they depend on me."

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स तदा दूतः पाण्डुपुत्रेण धीमता ।जगाम तत्र यत्रास्ते देवराजः शतक्रतुः ॥५३॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa tadā dūtaḥ pāṇḍuputreṇa dhīmatā ,jagāma tatra yatrāste devarājaḥ śatakratuḥ.
english: Thus addressed by the intelligent son of Pāṇḍu, the messenger then went to where the king of the gods, Śatakratu, was dwelling.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 53. Thus addressed by the intelligent son of Pāṇḍu, the messenger then went to where the king of the gods, Śatakratu, was dwelling.

sanskrit: निवेदयामास च तद्धर्मराजचिकीर्षितम् ।यथोक्तं धर्मपुत्रेण सर्वमेव जनाधिप ॥५४॥
transliteration: nivedayāmāsa ca taddharmarājacikīrṣitam ,yathoktaṁ dharmaputreṇa sarvameva janādhipa.
english: And he reported Dharmarāja's intention, everything as stated by the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), O ruler of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 54. And he reported Dharmarāja's intention, everything as stated by the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), O ruler of men.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स्थिते मुहूर्तं पार्थे तु धर्मराजे युधिष्ठिरे ।आजग्मुस्तत्र कौरव्य देवाः शक्रपुरोगमाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sthite muhūrtaṁ pārthe tu dharmarāje yudhiṣṭhire ,ājagmustatra kauravya devāḥ śakrapurogamāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: While Pārtha and King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), remained there for a moment, O scion of Kuru, the gods, led by Indra, arrived there.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: While Pārtha and King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), remained there for a moment, O scion of Kuru, the gods, led by Indra, arrived there.

sanskrit: स्वयं विग्रहवान्धर्मो राजानं प्रसमीक्षितुम् ।तत्राजगाम यत्रासौ कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥२॥
transliteration: svayaṁ vigrahavāndharmo rājānaṁ prasamīkṣitum ,tatrājagāma yatrāsau kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Natural law (dharma) itself, in embodied form, arrived there to observe the king, specifically where that King Yudhiṣṭhira, the Kuru monarch, was present.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. Natural law (dharma) itself, in embodied form, arrived there to observe the king, specifically where that King Yudhiṣṭhira, the Kuru monarch, was present.

sanskrit: तेषु भास्वरदेहेषु पुण्याभिजनकर्मसु ।समागतेषु देवेषु व्यगमत्तत्तमो नृप ॥३॥
transliteration: teṣu bhāsvaradeheṣu puṇyābhijanakarmasu ,samāgateṣu deveṣu vyagamattattamo nṛpa.
english: O King, as those gods arrived, possessing lustrous bodies and performing actions born of merit, that darkness completely disappeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. O King, as those gods arrived, possessing lustrous bodies and performing actions born of merit, that darkness completely disappeared.

sanskrit: नादृश्यन्त च तास्तत्र यातनाः पापकर्मिणाम् ।नदी वैतरणी चैव कूटशाल्मलिना सह ॥४॥
transliteration: nādṛśyanta ca tāstatra yātanāḥ pāpakarmiṇām ,nadī vaitaraṇī caiva kūṭaśālmalinā saha.
english: And there, those torments of the evildoers, the river Vaitaraṇī, and even the Kuṭaśālmali tree along with it, were no longer visible.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. And there, those torments of the evildoers, the river Vaitaraṇī, and even the Kuṭaśālmali tree along with it, were no longer visible.

sanskrit: लोहकुम्भ्यः शिलाश्चैव नादृश्यन्त भयानकाः ।विकृतानि शरीराणि यानि तत्र समन्ततः ।ददर्श राजा कौन्तेयस्तान्यदृश्यानि चाभवन् ॥५॥
transliteration: lohakumbhyaḥ śilāścaiva nādṛśyanta bhayānakāḥ ,vikṛtāni śarīrāṇi yāni tatra samantataḥ ,dadarśa rājā kaunteyastānyadṛśyāni cābhavan.
english: Terrifying iron pots and stones were not visible. King Kaunteya, however, saw disfigured bodies all around, and then they became invisible.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. Terrifying iron pots and stones were not visible. King Kaunteya, however, saw disfigured bodies all around, and then they became invisible.

sanskrit: ततो वायुः सुखस्पर्शः पुण्यगन्धवहः शिवः ।ववौ देवसमीपस्थः शीतलोऽतीव भारत ॥६॥
transliteration: tato vāyuḥ sukhasparśaḥ puṇyagandhavahaḥ śivaḥ ,vavau devasamīpasthaḥ śītalo'tīva bhārata.
english: Then, O Bhārata, a very cool wind, pleasant to the touch, carrying a sacred fragrance, and auspicious, blew near the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. Then, O Bhārata, a very cool wind, pleasant to the touch, carrying a sacred fragrance, and auspicious, blew near the gods.

sanskrit: मरुतः सह शक्रेण वसवश्चाश्विनौ सह ।साध्या रुद्रास्तथादित्या ये चान्येऽपि दिवौकसः ॥७॥
transliteration: marutaḥ saha śakreṇa vasavaścāśvinau saha ,sādhyā rudrāstathādityā ye cānye'pi divaukasaḥ.
english: The Maruts, with Shakra (Indra), and the Vasus with the two Ashvins, as well as the Sadhyas, the Rudras, and the Adityas, and also any other dwellers of heaven (divaukasaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. The Maruts, with Shakra (Indra), and the Vasus with the two Ashvins, as well as the Sadhyas, the Rudras, and the Adityas, and also any other dwellers of heaven (divaukasaḥ).

sanskrit: सर्वे तत्र समाजग्मुः सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः ।यत्र राजा महातेजा धर्मपुत्रः स्थितोऽभवत् ॥८॥
transliteration: sarve tatra samājagmuḥ siddhāśca paramarṣayaḥ ,yatra rājā mahātejā dharmaputraḥ sthito'bhavat.
english: All of them assembled there - the Siddhas and the great sages - at the place where the greatly effulgent King, Dharmaputra (son of dharma), was present.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. All of them assembled there - the Siddhas and the great sages - at the place where the greatly effulgent King, Dharmaputra (son of dharma), was present.

sanskrit: ततः शक्रः सुरपतिः श्रिया परमया युतः ।युधिष्ठिरमुवाचेदं सान्त्वपूर्वमिदं वचः ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ śakraḥ surapatiḥ śriyā paramayā yutaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiramuvācedaṁ sāntvapūrvamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Then Indra, the lord of the gods, endowed with supreme splendor, spoke these consoling words to Yudhishthira.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. Then Indra, the lord of the gods, endowed with supreme splendor, spoke these consoling words to Yudhishthira.

transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira mahābāho prītā devagaṇāstava ,ehyehi puruṣavyāghra kṛtametāvatā vibho ,siddhiḥ prāptā tvayā rājaँllokāścāpyakṣayāstava.
english: O Yudhishthira, O mighty-armed one, the hosts of gods are pleased with you. Come, come, O tiger among men, this much has been accomplished, O powerful one. Success has been attained by you, O King, and your worlds are indeed imperishable.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. O Yudhishthira, O mighty-armed one, the hosts of gods are pleased with you. sanskrit: न च मन्युस्त्वया कार्यः शृणु चेदं वचो मम ।अवश्यं नरकस्तात द्रष्टव्यः सर्वराजभिः ॥११॥

english: And you should not harbor any resentment. Listen, O dear one, to this word of mine. Indeed, hell (naraka) must certainly be seen by all kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. And you should not harbor any resentment. Listen, O dear one, to this word of mine. Indeed, hell (naraka) must certainly be seen by all kings.

sanskrit: शुभानामशुभानां च द्वौ राशी पुरुषर्षभ ।यः पूर्वं सुकृतं भुङ्क्ते पश्चान्निरयमेति सः ।पूर्वं नरकभाग्यस्तु पश्चात्स्वर्गमुपैति सः ॥१२॥
transliteration: śubhānāmaśubhānāṁ ca dvau rāśī puruṣarṣabha ,yaḥ pūrvaṁ sukṛtaṁ bhuṅkte paścānnirayameti saḥ ,pūrvaṁ narakabhāgyastu paścātsvargamupaiti saḥ.
english: O foremost of men, there are two accumulations: of good deeds and of bad deeds. He who first experiences the fruits of good deeds later goes to hell (niraya). But he who first experiences hell (naraka) afterwards attains heaven.

sanskrit: भूयिष्ठं पापकर्मा यः स पूर्वं स्वर्गमश्नुते ।तेन त्वमेवं गमितो मया श्रेयोर्थिना नृप ॥१३॥
transliteration: bhūyiṣṭhaṁ pāpakarmā yaḥ sa pūrvaṁ svargamaśnute ,tena tvamevaṁ gamito mayā śreyorthinā nṛpa.
english: The one who commits many sinful deeds first attains heaven. Therefore, O king, I, desiring your ultimate welfare, caused you to come here in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. The one who commits many sinful deeds first attains heaven. Therefore, O king, I, desiring your ultimate welfare, caused you to come here in this manner.

sanskrit: व्याजेन हि त्वया द्रोण उपचीर्णः सुतं प्रति ।व्याजेनैव ततो राजन्दर्शितो नरकस्तव ॥१४॥
transliteration: vyājena hi tvayā droṇa upacīrṇaḥ sutaṁ prati ,vyājenaiva tato rājandarśito narakastava.
english: Indeed, Drona was deceived by you through trickery regarding his son. Therefore, O king, hell was revealed to you precisely through that same deceit.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. Indeed, Drona was deceived by you through trickery regarding his son. Therefore, O king, hell was revealed to you precisely through that same deceit.

sanskrit: यथैव त्वं तथा भीमस्तथा पार्थो यमौ तथा ।द्रौपदी च तथा कृष्णा व्याजेन नरकं गताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: yathaiva tvaṁ tathā bhīmastathā pārtho yamau tathā ,draupadī ca tathā kṛṣṇā vyājena narakaṁ gatāḥ.
english: Just as you, so too Bhima, Arjuna, and the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Draupadi, also known as Kṛṣṇā, have similarly gone to hell due to deceit.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. Just as you, so too Bhima, Arjuna, and the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Draupadi, also known as Kṛṣṇā, have similarly gone to hell due to deceit.

sanskrit: आगच्छ नरशार्दूल मुक्तास्ते चैव किल्बिषात् ।स्वपक्षाश्चैव ये तुभ्यं पार्थिवा निहता रणे ।सर्वे स्वर्गमनुप्राप्तास्तान्पश्य पुरुषर्षभ ॥१६॥
transliteration: āgaccha naraśārdūla muktāste caiva kilbiṣāt ,svapakṣāścaiva ye tubhyaṁ pārthivā nihatā raṇe ,sarve svargamanuprāptāstānpaśya puruṣarṣabha.
english: Come, O best of men! They (the Pāṇḍavas and Draupadī) are now freed from sin. And all those kings of your side who were slain in battle have also attained heaven. See them, O bull among men!
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. Come, O best of men! They (the Pāṇḍavas and Draupadī) are now freed from sin. And all those kings of your side who were slain in battle have also attained heaven. See them, O bull among men!

sanskrit: कर्णश्चैव महेष्वासः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ।स गतः परमां सिद्धिं यदर्थं परितप्यसे ॥१७॥
transliteration: karṇaścaiva maheṣvāsaḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,sa gataḥ paramāṁ siddhiṁ yadarthaṁ paritapyase.
english: Karna, the great archer and the best among all wielders of weapons, has attained his supreme destiny, for whose sake you lament.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. Karna, the great archer and the best among all wielders of weapons, has attained his supreme destiny, for whose sake you lament.

sanskrit: तं पश्य पुरुषव्याघ्रमादित्यतनयं विभो ।स्वस्थानस्थं महाबाहो जहि शोकं नरर्षभ ॥१८॥
transliteration: taṁ paśya puruṣavyāghramādityatanayaṁ vibho ,svasthānasthaṁ mahābāho jahi śokaṁ nararṣabha.
english: O mighty one (vibho), behold him, the tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the son of the sun, Karna, now established in his own place. O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), abandon your grief!
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 18. O mighty one (vibho), behold him, the tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the son of the sun, Karna, now established in his own place. O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), abandon your grief!

sanskrit: भ्रातॄंश्चान्यांस्तथा पश्य स्वपक्षांश्चैव पार्थिवान् ।स्वं स्वं स्थानमनुप्राप्तान्व्येतु ते मानसो ज्वरः ॥१९॥

english: And also behold your brothers and other kings from your own faction, each having attained their own rightful place. Let your mental fever (mānaso jvara) depart.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 19. And also behold your brothers and other kings from your own faction, each having attained their own rightful place. Let your mental fever (mānaso jvara) depart.

sanskrit: अनुभूय पूर्वं त्वं कृच्छ्रमितः प्रभृति कौरव ।विहरस्व मया सार्धं गतशोको निरामयः ॥२०॥

english: O Kuru (kaurava), having previously endured hardship, from now on, free from sorrow and untroubled, enjoy yourself with me.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 20. O Kuru (kaurava), having previously endured hardship, from now on, free from sorrow and untroubled, enjoy yourself with me.

sanskrit: कर्मणां तात पुण्यानां जितानां तपसा स्वयम् ।दानानां च महाबाहो फलं प्राप्नुहि पाण्डव ॥२१॥

english: O dear one, O mighty-armed Pāṇḍava, attain the fruit of meritorious actions (karma), of those (achievements) gained through your own asceticism (tapas), and of your donations (dāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 21. O dear one, O mighty-armed Pāṇḍava, attain the fruit of meritorious actions (karma), of those (achievements) gained through your own asceticism (tapas), and of your donations (dāna).

sanskrit: अद्य त्वां देवगन्धर्वा दिव्याश्चाप्सरसो दिवि ।उपसेवन्तु कल्याणं विरजोम्बरवाससः ॥२२॥
transliteration: adya tvāṁ devagandharvā divyāścāpsaraso divi ,upasevantu kalyāṇaṁ virajombaravāsasaḥ.
english: Today, O blessed one, may the divine Gandharvas and celestial Apsarases, wearing spotless garments, attend upon you in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 22. Today, O blessed one, may the divine Gandharvas and celestial Apsarases, wearing spotless garments, attend upon you in heaven.

english: O mighty-armed one, may you attain the worlds won through the Rājasūya ritual and augmented by the Aśvamedha ritual, along with the great reward of your asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 23. O mighty-armed one, may you attain the worlds won through the Rājasūya ritual and augmented by the Aśvamedha ritual, along with the great reward of your asceticism (tapas).

search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 24. Indeed, O Yudhiṣṭhira, O son of Pṛthā, your realms will be superior to (those of) all kings, just like those of Hariścandra, and in them you shall rejoice.

english: Where the royal sage Māndhātā resided, where King Bhagīratha resided, and where Bharata, son of Duḥṣanta, resided, there you too shall dwell.

english: O Pārtha, this is the sacred divine river, the purifier of the three worlds, the celestial Gaṅgā. O King of kings, having bathed in it there, you will proceed.

sanskrit: अत्र स्नातस्य ते भावो मानुषो विगमिष्यति ।गतशोको निरायासो मुक्तवैरो भविष्यसि ॥२७॥

transliteration: evaṁ bruvati devendre kauravendraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,dharmo vigrahavānsākṣāduvāca sutamātmanaḥ.
english: As the lord of the gods spoke thus to Yudhiṣṭhira, the chief of the Kauravas, the principle of natural law (dharma) in embodied form directly spoke to his own son.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 28. As the lord of the gods spoke thus to Yudhiṣṭhira, the chief of the Kauravas, the principle of natural law (dharma) in embodied form directly spoke to his own son.

sanskrit: भो भो राजन्महाप्राज्ञ प्रीतोऽस्मि तव पुत्रक ।मद्भक्त्या सत्यवाक्येन क्षमया च दमेन च ॥२९॥
transliteration: bho bho rājanmahāprājña prīto'smi tava putraka ,madbhaktyā satyavākyena kṣamayā ca damena ca.
english: O King, O greatly wise one, my dear son, I am pleased by your devotion (bhakti) to me, your truthfulness of speech, your forgiveness, and your self-control.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 29. O King, O greatly wise one, my dear son, I am pleased by your devotion (bhakti) to me, your truthfulness of speech, your forgiveness, and your self-control.
