transliteration: eṣā tṛtīyā jijñāsā tava rājankṛtā mayā ,na śakyase cālayituṁ svabhāvātpārtha hetubhiḥ.
english: O King, this third inquiry has been put forth by me to you. O Pārtha, you cannot be swayed from your intrinsic nature (svabhāva) by various reasons.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 30. O King, this third inquiry has been put forth by me to you. O Pārtha, you cannot be swayed from your intrinsic nature (svabhāva) by various reasons.

sanskrit: पूर्वं परीक्षितो हि त्वमासीर्द्वैतवनं प्रति ।अरणीसहितस्यार्थे तच्च निस्तीर्णवानसि ॥३१॥
transliteration: pūrvaṁ parīkṣito hi tvamāsīrdvaitavanaṁ prati ,araṇīsahitasyārthe tacca nistīrṇavānasi.
english: Indeed, you were tested before regarding the Dvaita forest, for the sake of the araṇi (fire-drill) wood; and that, too, you successfully accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 31. Indeed, you were tested before regarding the Dvaita forest, for the sake of the araṇi (fire-drill) wood; and that, too, you successfully accomplished.

sanskrit: सोदर्येषु विनष्टेषु द्रौपद्यां तत्र भारत ।श्वरूपधारिणा पुत्र पुनस्त्वं मे परीक्षितः ॥३२॥
transliteration: sodaryeṣu vinaṣṭeṣu draupadyāṁ tatra bhārata ,śvarūpadhāriṇā putra punastvaṁ me parīkṣitaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, when your brothers (sodarya) had perished and Draupadī was present there, O son, you were again tested by me, who appeared in the form of a dog.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 32. O Bhārata, when your brothers (sodarya) had perished and Draupadī was present there, O son, you were again tested by me, who appeared in the form of a dog.

sanskrit: इदं तृतीयं भ्रातॄणामर्थे यत्स्थातुमिच्छसि ।विशुद्धोऽसि महाभाग सुखी विगतकल्मषः ॥३३॥
transliteration: idaṁ tṛtīyaṁ bhrātṝṇāmarthe yatsthātumicchasi ,viśuddho'si mahābhāga sukhī vigatakalmaṣaḥ.
english: This third [suffering] for the sake of your brothers, which you wish to endure. You are purified, O highly blessed one, happy and freed from all defilement.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 33. This third [suffering] for the sake of your brothers, which you wish to endure. You are purified, O highly blessed one, happy and freed from all defilement.

sanskrit: न च ते भ्रातरः पार्थ नरकस्था विशां पते ।मायैषा देवराजेन महेन्द्रेण प्रयोजिता ॥३४॥
transliteration: na ca te bhrātaraḥ pārtha narakasthā viśāṁ pate ,māyaiṣā devarājena mahendreṇa prayojitā.
english: And your brothers are not in hell (naraka), O Pārtha, O lord of subjects. This is an illusion (māyā) created by Mahendra, the king of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 34. And your brothers are not in hell (naraka), O Pārtha, O lord of subjects. This is an illusion (māyā) created by Mahendra, the king of the gods.

sanskrit: अवश्यं नरकस्तात द्रष्टव्यः सर्वराजभिः ।ततस्त्वया प्राप्तमिदं मुहूर्तं दुःखमुत्तमम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: avaśyaṁ narakastāta draṣṭavyaḥ sarvarājabhiḥ ,tatastvayā prāptamidaṁ muhūrtaṁ duḥkhamuttamam.
english: Certainly, O dear one, hell (naraka) must be experienced by all kings. Therefore, this great and momentary suffering has been undergone by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 35. Certainly, O dear one, hell (naraka) must be experienced by all kings. Therefore, this great and momentary suffering has been undergone by you.

sanskrit: न सव्यसाची भीमो वा यमौ वा पुरुषर्षभौ ।कर्णो वा सत्यवाक्शूरो नरकार्हाश्चिरं नृप ॥३६॥
transliteration: na savyasācī bhīmo vā yamau vā puruṣarṣabhau ,karṇo vā satyavākśūro narakārhāściraṁ nṛpa.
english: Neither Savyasācin, nor Bhīma, nor the two foremost of men (the twins), nor Karṇa, the truthful and heroic one, are deserving of hell (naraka) for long, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 36. Neither Savyasācin, nor Bhīma, nor the two foremost of men (the twins), nor Karṇa, the truthful and heroic one, are deserving of hell (naraka) for long, O king.

sanskrit: न कृष्णा राजपुत्री च नरकार्हा युधिष्ठिर ।एह्येहि भरतश्रेष्ठ पश्य गङ्गां त्रिलोकगाम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: na kṛṣṇā rājaputrī ca narakārhā yudhiṣṭhira ,ehyehi bharataśreṣṭha paśya gaṅgāṁ trilokagām.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, even the princess Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) is not deserving of hell. Come, O best of the Bhāratas, and behold the Gaṅgā, which traverses the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 37. O Yudhiṣṭhira, even the princess Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) is not deserving of hell. Come, O best of the Bhāratas, and behold the Gaṅgā, which traverses the three worlds.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स राजर्षिस्तव पूर्वपितामहः ।जगाम सह धर्मेण सर्वैश्च त्रिदशालयैः ॥३८॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa rājarṣistava pūrvapitāmahaḥ ,jagāma saha dharmeṇa sarvaiśca tridaśālayaiḥ.
english: Thus addressed, that royal sage, your great-grandfather, departed along with (dharma) and all the divine beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 38. Thus addressed, that royal sage, your great-grandfather, departed along with (dharma) and all the divine beings.

sanskrit: गङ्गां देवनदीं पुण्यां पावनीमृषिसंस्तुताम् ।अवगाह्य तु तां राजा तनुं तत्याज मानुषीम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: gaṅgāṁ devanadīṁ puṇyāṁ pāvanīmṛṣisaṁstutām ,avagāhya tu tāṁ rājā tanuṁ tatyāja mānuṣīm.
english: Having plunged into that Gaṅgā, the divine river, sacred, purifying, and praised by sages, the king then abandoned his human body.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 39. Having plunged into that Gaṅgā, the divine river, sacred, purifying, and praised by sages, the king then abandoned his human body.

sanskrit: ततो दिव्यवपुर्भूत्वा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।निर्वैरो गतसंतापो जले तस्मिन्समाप्लुतः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tato divyavapurbhūtvā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,nirvairo gatasaṁtāpo jale tasminsamāplutaḥ.
english: Thereafter, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of (dharma), having assumed a divine body and being free from enmity and grief, plunged into that water.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 40. Thereafter, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of (dharma), having assumed a divine body and being free from enmity and grief, plunged into that water.

sanskrit: ततो ययौ वृतो देवैः कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।धर्मेण सहितो धीमान्स्तूयमानो महर्षिभिः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tato yayau vṛto devaiḥ kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,dharmeṇa sahito dhīmānstūyamāno maharṣibhiḥ.
english: Then, the wise king of the Kurus, Yudhiṣṭhira, proceeded, accompanied by his natural law (dharma), surrounded by the gods and praised by the great sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 41. Then, the wise king of the Kurus, Yudhiṣṭhira, proceeded, accompanied by his natural law (dharma), surrounded by the gods and praised by the great sages.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा देवैः सर्षिमरुद्गणैः ।पूज्यमानो ययौ तत्र यत्र ते कुरुपुंगवाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā devaiḥ sarṣimarudgaṇaiḥ ,pūjyamāno yayau tatra yatra te kurupuṁgavāḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, being honored by the gods, along with groups of sages and Maruts, proceeded to the place where those eminent Kuru heroes were.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, being honored by the gods, along with groups of sages and Maruts, proceeded to the place where those eminent Kuru heroes were.

sanskrit: ददर्श तत्र गोविन्दं ब्राह्मेण वपुषान्वितम् ।तेनैव दृष्टपूर्वेण सादृश्येनोपसूचितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: dadarśa tatra govindaṁ brāhmeṇa vapuṣānvitam ,tenaiva dṛṣṭapūrveṇa sādṛśyenopasūcitam.
english: There he beheld Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), endowed with a divine body (brahman), which was indicated by the same form he had previously seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. There he beheld Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), endowed with a divine body (brahman), which was indicated by the same form he had previously seen.

sanskrit: दीप्यमानं स्ववपुषा दिव्यैरस्त्रैरुपस्थितम् ।चक्रप्रभृतिभिर्घोरैर्दिव्यैः पुरुषविग्रहैः ।उपास्यमानं वीरेण फल्गुनेन सुवर्चसा ॥३॥
transliteration: dīpyamānaṁ svavapuṣā divyairastrairupasthitam ,cakraprabhṛtibhirghorairdivyaiḥ puruṣavigrahaiḥ ,upāsyamānaṁ vīreṇa phalgunena suvarcasā.
english: He was shining with his own body, attended by formidable divine weapons like the discus (cakra) and others, which appeared as divine, personified beings (puruṣa-vigrahaiḥ). He was also being served by the glorious hero Phalguna (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. He was shining with his own body, attended by formidable divine weapons like the discus (cakra) and others, which appeared as divine, personified beings (puruṣa-vigrahaiḥ). He was also being served by the glorious hero Phalguna (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अपरस्मिन्नथोद्देशे कर्णं शस्त्रभृतां वरम् ।द्वादशादित्यसहितं ददर्श कुरुनन्दनः ॥४॥
transliteration: aparasminnathoddeśe karṇaṁ śastrabhṛtāṁ varam ,dvādaśādityasahitaṁ dadarśa kurunandanaḥ.
english: Then, in another direction, Yudhishthira (the delight of the Kurus) saw Karna, the foremost of weapon-bearers, accompanied by the twelve Adityas.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. Then, in another direction, Yudhishthira (the delight of the Kurus) saw Karna, the foremost of weapon-bearers, accompanied by the twelve Adityas.

sanskrit: अथापरस्मिन्नुद्देशे मरुद्गणवृतं प्रभुम् ।भीमसेनमथापश्यत्तेनैव वपुषान्वितम् ॥५॥
transliteration: athāparasminnuddeśe marudgaṇavṛtaṁ prabhum ,bhīmasenamathāpaśyattenaiva vapuṣānvitam.
english: Then, in another direction, he also saw the mighty Bhimasena, surrounded by the hosts of Maruts and endowed with that very same form.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. Then, in another direction, he also saw the mighty Bhimasena, surrounded by the hosts of Maruts and endowed with that very same form.

sanskrit: अश्विनोस्तु तथा स्थाने दीप्यमानौ स्वतेजसा ।नकुलं सहदेवं च ददर्श कुरुनन्दनः ॥६॥
transliteration: aśvinostu tathā sthāne dīpyamānau svatejasā ,nakulaṁ sahadevaṁ ca dadarśa kurunandanaḥ.
english: And similarly, in the place corresponding to the Ashvins, Yudhishthira (the delight of the Kurus) saw Nakula and Sahadeva, both glowing with their own radiance.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. And similarly, in the place corresponding to the Ashvins, Yudhishthira (the delight of the Kurus) saw Nakula and Sahadeva, both glowing with their own radiance.

sanskrit: तथा ददर्श पाञ्चालीं कमलोत्पलमालिनीम् ।वपुषा स्वर्गमाक्रम्य तिष्ठन्तीमर्कवर्चसम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tathā dadarśa pāñcālīṁ kamalotpalamālinīm ,vapuṣā svargamākramya tiṣṭhantīmarkavarcasam.
english: Then he also saw Panchali (Draupadi), adorned with garlands of lotuses and water lilies, appearing to occupy the heavens with her form, and shining with the brilliance of the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. Then he also saw Panchali (Draupadi), adorned with garlands of lotuses and water lilies, appearing to occupy the heavens with her form, and shining with the brilliance of the sun.

sanskrit: अथैनां सहसा राजा प्रष्टुमैच्छद्युधिष्ठिरः ।ततोऽस्य भगवानिन्द्रः कथयामास देवराट् ॥८॥
transliteration: athaināṁ sahasā rājā praṣṭumaicchadyudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,tato'sya bhagavānindraḥ kathayāmāsa devarāṭ.
english: Then King Yudhishthira suddenly wished to question her. Subsequently, the venerable Indra, the king of the gods, narrated (the story) to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. Then King Yudhishthira suddenly wished to question her. Subsequently, the venerable Indra, the king of the gods, narrated (the story) to him.

sanskrit: श्रीरेषा द्रौपदीरूपा त्वदर्थे मानुषं गता ।अयोनिजा लोककान्ता पुण्यगन्धा युधिष्ठिर ॥९॥
transliteration: śrīreṣā draupadīrūpā tvadarthe mānuṣaṁ gatā ,ayonijā lokakāntā puṇyagandhā yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhishthira, this (woman) in the form of Draupadi is Śrī herself, who has come into the human world for your sake. She is not womb-born, is beloved by all people, and possesses a sacred fragrance.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. O Yudhishthira, this (woman) in the form of Draupadi is Śrī herself, who has come into the human world for your sake. She is not womb-born, is beloved by all people, and possesses a sacred fragrance.

sanskrit: द्रुपदस्य कुले जाता भवद्भिश्चोपजीविता ।रत्यर्थं भवतां ह्येषा निर्मिता शूलपाणिना ॥१०॥
transliteration: drupadasya kule jātā bhavadbhiścopajīvitā ,ratyarthaṁ bhavatāṁ hyeṣā nirmitā śūlapāṇinā.
english: She was born in the family of Drupada and has been sustained by all of you. Indeed, this woman was created by Śūlapāṇi (Śiva) for your delight.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. She was born in the family of Drupada and has been sustained by all of you. Indeed, this woman was created by Śūlapāṇi (Śiva) for your delight.

sanskrit: एते पञ्च महाभागा गन्धर्वाः पावकप्रभाः ।द्रौपद्यास्तनया राजन्युष्माकममितौजसः ॥११॥
transliteration: ete pañca mahābhāgā gandharvāḥ pāvakaprabhāḥ ,draupadyāstanayā rājanyuṣmākamamitaujasaḥ.
english: O king, these five highly fortunate Gandharvas, who possess the splendor of fire, are Draupadi's sons, endowed with immeasurable prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. O king, these five highly fortunate Gandharvas, who possess the splendor of fire, are Draupadi's sons, endowed with immeasurable prowess.

sanskrit: पश्य गन्धर्वराजानं धृतराष्ट्रं मनीषिणम् ।एनं च त्वं विजानीहि भ्रातरं पूर्वजं पितुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: paśya gandharvarājānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ manīṣiṇam ,enaṁ ca tvaṁ vijānīhi bhrātaraṁ pūrvajaṁ pituḥ.
english: Behold Dhritarashtra, the wise king of the Gandharvas. And you, know this one as your father's elder brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. Behold Dhritarashtra, the wise king of the Gandharvas. And you, know this one as your father's elder brother.

sanskrit: अयं ते पूर्वजो भ्राता कौन्तेयः पावकद्युतिः ।सूर्यपुत्रोऽग्रजः श्रेष्ठो राधेय इति विश्रुतः ।आदित्यसहितो याति पश्यैनं पुरुषर्षभ ॥१३॥
transliteration: ayaṁ te pūrvajo bhrātā kaunteyaḥ pāvakadyutiḥ ,sūryaputro'grajaḥ śreṣṭho rādheya iti viśrutaḥ ,ādityasahito yāti paśyainaṁ puruṣarṣabha.
english: This is your elder brother, O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), whose brilliance is like fire. He is the son of Surya, the eldest, the most excellent, and renowned as Radheya. He moves accompanied by the Adityas; O best of men, behold him.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. This is your elder brother, O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), whose brilliance is like fire. He is the son of Surya, the eldest, the most excellent, and renowned as Radheya. He moves accompanied by the Adityas; O best of men, behold him.

sanskrit: साध्यानामथ देवानां वसूनां मरुतामपि ।गणेषु पश्य राजेन्द्र वृष्ण्यन्धकमहारथान् ।सात्यकिप्रमुखान्वीरान्भोजांश्चैव महारथान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: sādhyānāmatha devānāṁ vasūnāṁ marutāmapi ,gaṇeṣu paśya rājendra vṛṣṇyandhakamahārathān ,sātyakipramukhānvīrānbhojāṁścaiva mahārathān.
english: O king of kings (rājendra), behold among the multitudes of Sadhyas, gods, Vasus, and Maruts, the great charioteers of the Vrishnis and Andhakas. See also the heroes led by Satyaki, and indeed the great charioteer Bhojas.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. O king of kings (rājendra), behold among the multitudes of Sadhyas, gods, Vasus, and Maruts, the great charioteers of the Vrishnis and Andhakas. See also the heroes led by Satyaki, and indeed the great charioteer Bhojas.

sanskrit: सोमेन सहितं पश्य सौभद्रमपराजितम् ।अभिमन्युं महेष्वासं निशाकरसमद्युतिम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: somena sahitaṁ paśya saubhadramaparājitam ,abhimanyuṁ maheṣvāsaṁ niśākarasamadyutim.
english: Behold the unconquered son of Subhadra, Abhimanyu, accompanied by Soma. He is a great archer whose radiance is like that of the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. Behold the unconquered son of Subhadra, Abhimanyu, accompanied by Soma. He is a great archer whose radiance is like that of the moon.

sanskrit: एष पाण्डुर्महेष्वासः कुन्त्या माद्र्या च संगतः ।विमानेन सदाभ्येति पिता तव ममान्तिकम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: eṣa pāṇḍurmaheṣvāsaḥ kuntyā mādryā ca saṁgataḥ ,vimānena sadābhyeti pitā tava mamāntikam.
english: This great archer Pāṇḍu, your father, accompanied by Kuntī and Mādrī, always approaches my presence by means of an aerial vehicle.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 16. This great archer Pāṇḍu, your father, accompanied by Kuntī and Mādrī, always approaches my presence by means of an aerial vehicle.

sanskrit: वसुभिः सहितं पश्य भीष्मं शांतनवं नृपम् ।द्रोणं बृहस्पतेः पार्श्वे गुरुमेनं निशामय ॥१७॥
transliteration: vasubhiḥ sahitaṁ paśya bhīṣmaṁ śāṁtanavaṁ nṛpam ,droṇaṁ bṛhaspateḥ pārśve gurumenaṁ niśāmaya.
english: Behold King Bhīṣma, son of Śāntanu, accompanied by the Vasus. And observe this teacher Droṇa beside Bṛhaspati.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 17. Behold King Bhīṣma, son of Śāntanu, accompanied by the Vasus. And observe this teacher Droṇa beside Bṛhaspati.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये महीपाला योधास्तव च पाण्डव ।गन्धर्वैः सहिता यान्ति यक्षैः पुण्यजनैस्तथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: ete cānye mahīpālā yodhāstava ca pāṇḍava ,gandharvaiḥ sahitā yānti yakṣaiḥ puṇyajanaistathā.
english: O Pāṇḍava, these and other kings, and your warriors too, accompanied by Gandharvas, Yakṣas, and Puṇyajanas, are going (to the heavens).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 18. O Pāṇḍava, these and other kings, and your warriors too, accompanied by Gandharvas, Yakṣas, and Puṇyajanas, are going (to the heavens).

sanskrit: गुह्यकानां गतिं चापि केचित्प्राप्ता नृसत्तमाः ।त्यक्त्वा देहं जितस्वर्गाः पुण्यवाग्बुद्धिकर्मभिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: guhyakānāṁ gatiṁ cāpi kecitprāptā nṛsattamāḥ ,tyaktvā dehaṁ jitasvargāḥ puṇyavāgbuddhikarmabhiḥ.
english: And some of the best among men, having abandoned their bodies and conquered the heavens, have also attained the state of the Guhyakas through their meritorious speech, intellect, and actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 19. And some of the best among men, having abandoned their bodies and conquered the heavens, have also attained the state of the Guhyakas through their meritorious speech, intellect, and actions.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।भीष्मद्रोणौ महात्मानौ धृतराष्ट्रश्च पार्थिवः ।विराटद्रुपदौ चोभौ शङ्खश्चैवोत्तरस्तथा ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,bhīṣmadroṇau mahātmānau dhṛtarāṣṭraśca pārthivaḥ ,virāṭadrupadau cobhau śaṅkhaścaivottarastathā.
english: Janamejaya said: Bhishma and Drona, the great-souled ones, and King Dhritarashtra; and both Virata and Drupada; and also Shankha, and Uttara.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: Bhishma and Drona, the great-souled ones, and King Dhritarashtra; and both Virata and Drupada; and also Shankha, and Uttara.

sanskrit: धृष्टकेतुर्जयत्सेनो राजा चैव स सत्यजित् ।दुर्योधनसुताश्चैव शकुनिश्चैव सौबलः ॥२॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭaketurjayatseno rājā caiva sa satyajit ,duryodhanasutāścaiva śakuniścaiva saubalaḥ.
english: Dhrishtaketu, Jayatsena, and also King Satyajit; and also the sons of Duryodhana, and Shakuni, the son of Subala.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. Dhrishtaketu, Jayatsena, and also King Satyajit; and also the sons of Duryodhana, and Shakuni, the son of Subala.

sanskrit: कर्णपुत्राश्च विक्रान्ता राजा चैव जयद्रथः ।घटोत्कचादयश्चैव ये चान्ये नानुकीर्तिताः ॥३॥
transliteration: karṇaputrāśca vikrāntā rājā caiva jayadrathaḥ ,ghaṭotkacādayaścaiva ye cānye nānukīrtitāḥ.
english: And the valiant sons of Karna, and also King Jayadratha; and Ghatotkacha and others, and those others who were not mentioned (here).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. And the valiant sons of Karna, and also King Jayadratha; and Ghatotkacha and others, and those others who were not mentioned (here).

sanskrit: ये चान्ये कीर्तितास्तत्र राजानो दीप्तमूर्तयः ।स्वर्गे कालं कियन्तं ते तस्थुस्तदपि शंस मे ॥४॥
transliteration: ye cānye kīrtitāstatra rājāno dīptamūrtayaḥ ,svarge kālaṁ kiyantaṁ te tasthustadapi śaṁsa me.
english: And those other kings with radiant forms who were mentioned there - how much time did they spend in heaven? Please tell me that also.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. And those other kings with radiant forms who were mentioned there - how much time did they spend in heaven? Please tell me that also.

sanskrit: आहोस्विच्छाश्वतं स्थानं तेषां तत्र द्विजोत्तम ।अन्ते वा कर्मणः कां ते गतिं प्राप्ता नरर्षभाः ।एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं प्रोच्यमानं त्वया द्विज ॥५॥
transliteration: āhosvicchāśvataṁ sthānaṁ teṣāṁ tatra dvijottama ,ante vā karmaṇaḥ kāṁ te gatiṁ prāptā nararṣabhāḥ ,etadicchāmyahaṁ śrotuṁ procyamānaṁ tvayā dvija.
english: O best among the twice-born (dvija), did they attain an eternal abode there? Or, at the end of their actions (karma), what state did those eminent men achieve? This I wish to hear, told by you, O twice-born (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. O best among the twice-born (dvija), did they attain an eternal abode there? Or, at the end of their actions (karma), what state did those eminent men achieve? This I wish to hear, told by you, O twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: सूत उवाच ।इत्युक्तः स तु विप्रर्षिरनुज्ञातो महात्मना ।व्यासेन तस्य नृपतेराख्यातुमुपचक्रमे ॥६॥
transliteration: sūta uvāca ,ityuktaḥ sa tu viprarṣiranujñāto mahātmanā ,vyāsena tasya nṛpaterākhyātumupacakrame.
english: Suta said: Thus spoken to, that Brahmin sage, having been granted permission by the great-souled Vyasa, began to narrate to that king.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. Suta said: Thus spoken to, that Brahmin sage, having been granted permission by the great-souled Vyasa, began to narrate to that king.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।गन्तव्यं कर्मणामन्ते सर्वेण मनुजाधिप ।शृणु गुह्यमिदं राजन्देवानां भरतर्षभ ।यदुवाच महातेजा दिव्यचक्षुः प्रतापवान् ॥७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,gantavyaṁ karmaṇāmante sarveṇa manujādhipa ,śṛṇu guhyamidaṁ rājandevānāṁ bharatarṣabha ,yaduvāca mahātejā divyacakṣuḥ pratāpavān.
english: Vaishampayana said: O lord of men (manujādhipa), everyone must reach their destination at the culmination of their actions (karma). Listen, O king, O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), to this secret of the gods, which was spoken by the greatly effulgent, divinely-sighted, and powerful sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Vaishampayana said: O lord of men (manujādhipa), everyone must reach their destination at the culmination of their actions (karma). Listen, O king, O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), to this secret of the gods, which was spoken by the greatly effulgent, divinely-sighted, and powerful sage.

sanskrit: मुनिः पुराणः कौरव्य पाराशर्यो महाव्रतः ।अगाधबुद्धिः सर्वज्ञो गतिज्ञः सर्वकर्मणाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: muniḥ purāṇaḥ kauravya pārāśaryo mahāvrataḥ ,agādhabuddhiḥ sarvajño gatijñaḥ sarvakarmaṇām.
english: The ancient sage (muni), O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), Parasharya, who observed great vows, possessed unfathomable intellect, was omniscient, and knew the ultimate course of all actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. The ancient sage (muni), O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), Parasharya, who observed great vows, possessed unfathomable intellect, was omniscient, and knew the ultimate course of all actions (karma).

sanskrit: वसूनेव महातेजा भीष्मः प्राप महाद्युतिः ।अष्टावेव हि दृश्यन्ते वसवो भरतर्षभ ॥९॥
transliteration: vasūneva mahātejā bhīṣmaḥ prāpa mahādyutiḥ ,aṣṭāveva hi dṛśyante vasavo bharatarṣabha.
english: The greatly effulgent, highly splendid Bhishma indeed attained the Vasus. For, O best of the Bharatas, only eight Vasus are known to exist.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. The greatly effulgent, highly splendid Bhishma indeed attained the Vasus. For, O best of the Bharatas, only eight Vasus are known to exist.

sanskrit: बृहस्पतिं विवेशाथ द्रोणो ह्यङ्गिरसां वरम् ।कृतवर्मा तु हार्दिक्यः प्रविवेश मरुद्गणम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: bṛhaspatiṁ viveśātha droṇo hyaṅgirasāṁ varam ,kṛtavarmā tu hārdikyaḥ praviveśa marudgaṇam.
english: Then Drona indeed entered Brihaspati, who was the best of the Angirasas. But Kritavarma, the son of Hridika, entered the host of Maruts.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. Then Drona indeed entered Brihaspati, who was the best of the Angirasas. But Kritavarma, the son of Hridika, entered the host of Maruts.

sanskrit: सनत्कुमारं प्रद्युम्नः प्रविवेश यथागतम् ।धृतराष्ट्रो धनेशस्य लोकान्प्राप दुरासदान् ॥११॥
transliteration: sanatkumāraṁ pradyumnaḥ praviveśa yathāgatam ,dhṛtarāṣṭro dhaneśasya lokānprāpa durāsadān.
english: Pradyumna entered Sanatkumara, returning to his original form. Dhritarashtra attained the inaccessible worlds of Kubera, the lord of wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. Pradyumna entered Sanatkumara, returning to his original form. Dhritarashtra attained the inaccessible worlds of Kubera, the lord of wealth.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रेण सहिता गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ।पत्नीभ्यां सहितः पाण्डुर्महेन्द्रसदनं ययौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa sahitā gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī ,patnībhyāṁ sahitaḥ pāṇḍurmahendrasadanaṁ yayau.
english: Gandhari, the illustrious one, accompanied Dhritarashtra. And Pandu, accompanied by his two wives, went to the abode of Mahendra (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. Gandhari, the illustrious one, accompanied Dhritarashtra. And Pandu, accompanied by his two wives, went to the abode of Mahendra (Indra).

sanskrit: विराटद्रुपदौ चोभौ धृष्टकेतुश्च पार्थिवः ।निशठाक्रूरसाम्बाश्च भानुः कम्पो विडूरथः ॥१३॥
transliteration: virāṭadrupadau cobhau dhṛṣṭaketuśca pārthivaḥ ,niśaṭhākrūrasāmbāśca bhānuḥ kampo viḍūrathaḥ.
english: Both Virata and Drupada, and the king Dhristaketu; along with Nishatha, Akrura, Samba, and also Bhanu, Kampa, and Viduratha.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. Both Virata and Drupada, and the king Dhristaketu; along with Nishatha, Akrura, Samba, and also Bhanu, Kampa, and Viduratha.

sanskrit: भूरिश्रवाः शलश्चैव भूरिश्च पृथिवीपतिः ।उग्रसेनस्तथा कंसो वसुदेवश्च वीर्यवान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhūriśravāḥ śalaścaiva bhūriśca pṛthivīpatiḥ ,ugrasenastathā kaṁso vasudevaśca vīryavān.
english: Bhurishravas, Shala, and Bhuri, the king; Ugrasena, as well as Kamsa, and the mighty Vasudeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. Bhurishravas, Shala, and Bhuri, the king; Ugrasena, as well as Kamsa, and the mighty Vasudeva.

sanskrit: उत्तरश्च सह भ्रात्रा शङ्खेन नरपुंगवः ।विश्वेषां देवतानां ते विविशुर्नरसत्तमाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: uttaraśca saha bhrātrā śaṅkhena narapuṁgavaḥ ,viśveṣāṁ devatānāṁ te viviśurnarasattamāḥ.
english: And Uttara, the foremost among men, along with his brother Shankha - these excellent men entered the realm of all the deities.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. And Uttara, the foremost among men, along with his brother Shankha - these excellent men entered the realm of all the deities.

sanskrit: वर्चा नाम महातेजाः सोमपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।सोऽभिमन्युर्नृसिंहस्य फल्गुनस्य सुतोऽभवत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: varcā nāma mahātejāḥ somaputraḥ pratāpavān ,so'bhimanyurnṛsiṁhasya phalgunasya suto'bhavat.
english: Varcas by name, the greatly resplendent and powerful son of Soma, he became Abhimanyu, the son of the lion-like Phalguna (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. Varcas by name, the greatly resplendent and powerful son of Soma, he became Abhimanyu, the son of the lion-like Phalguna (Arjuna).

sanskrit: स युद्ध्वा क्षत्रधर्मेण यथा नान्यः पुमान्क्वचित् ।विवेश सोमं धर्मात्मा कर्मणोऽन्ते महारथः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa yuddhvā kṣatradharmeṇa yathā nānyaḥ pumānkvacit ,viveśa somaṁ dharmātmā karmaṇo'nte mahārathaḥ.
english: He, the great warrior and righteous-souled one (dharmātmā), having fought in accordance with the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a kṣatriya as no other man ever did, entered the lunar realm at the conclusion of his actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. He, the great warrior and righteous-souled one (dharmātmā), having fought in accordance with the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a kṣatriya as no other man ever did, entered the lunar realm at the conclusion of his actions.

sanskrit: आविवेश रविं कर्णः पितरं पुरुषर्षभ ।द्वापरं शकुनिः प्राप धृष्टद्युम्नस्तु पावकम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: āviveśa raviṁ karṇaḥ pitaraṁ puruṣarṣabha ,dvāparaṁ śakuniḥ prāpa dhṛṣṭadyumnastu pāvakam.
english: Karṇa, the best among men, entered the Sun, his father. Śakuni attained Dvāpara. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, however, entered fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. Karṇa, the best among men, entered the Sun, his father. Śakuni attained Dvāpara. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, however, entered fire.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रात्मजाः सर्वे यातुधाना बलोत्कटाः ।ऋद्धिमन्तो महात्मानः शस्त्रपूता दिवं गताः ।धर्ममेवाविशत्क्षत्ता राजा चैव युधिष्ठिरः ॥१९॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrātmajāḥ sarve yātudhānā balotkaṭāḥ ,ṛddhimanto mahātmānaḥ śastrapūtā divaṁ gatāḥ ,dharmamevāviśatkṣattā rājā caiva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: All the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who were exceedingly strong Rākṣasas, prosperous, and great-souled (mahātmā), purified by weapons, went to heaven. Vidura and King Yudhiṣṭhira, however, entered into natural law (dharma) itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. All the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who were exceedingly strong Rākṣasas, prosperous, and great-souled (mahātmā), purified by weapons, went to heaven. Vidura and King Yudhiṣṭhira, however, entered into natural law (dharma) itself.

sanskrit: अनन्तो भगवान्देवः प्रविवेश रसातलम् ।पितामहनियोगाद्धि यो योगाद्गामधारयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: ananto bhagavāndevaḥ praviveśa rasātalam ,pitāmahaniyogāddhi yo yogādgāmadhārayat.
english: The infinite, divine Lord Ananta entered Rasātala. Indeed, by the command of the Grandfather (Brahmā), it was he who sustained the earth by means of his mystic power (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. The infinite, divine Lord Ananta entered Rasātala. Indeed, by the command of the Grandfather (Brahmā), it was he who sustained the earth by means of his mystic power (yoga).

sanskrit: षोडशस्त्रीसहस्राणि वासुदेवपरिग्रहः ।न्यमज्जन्त सरस्वत्यां कालेन जनमेजय ।ताश्चाप्यप्सरसो भूत्वा वासुदेवमुपागमन् ॥२१॥
transliteration: ṣoḍaśastrīsahasrāṇi vāsudevaparigrahaḥ ,nyamajjanta sarasvatyāṁ kālena janamejaya ,tāścāpyapsaraso bhūtvā vāsudevamupāgaman.
english: O Janamejaya, in time, the sixteen thousand consorts of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa) immersed themselves in the Sarasvati river. They then became celestial nymphs (apsaras) and approached Vasudeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. O Janamejaya, in time, the sixteen thousand consorts of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa) immersed themselves in the Sarasvati river. They then became celestial nymphs (apsaras) and approached Vasudeva.

sanskrit: हतास्तस्मिन्महायुद्धे ये वीरास्तु महारथाः ।घटोत्कचादयः सर्वे देवान्यक्षांश्च भेजिरे ॥२२॥
transliteration: hatāstasminmahāyuddhe ye vīrāstu mahārathāḥ ,ghaṭotkacādayaḥ sarve devānyakṣāṁśca bhejire.
english: All those heroes and great warriors (mahārathas), such as Ghaṭotkaca and others, who were slain in that great war, ascended to the gods and the yakṣas.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. All those heroes and great warriors (mahārathas), such as Ghaṭotkaca and others, who were slain in that great war, ascended to the gods and the yakṣas.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनसहायाश्च राक्षसाः परिकीर्तिताः ।प्राप्तास्ते क्रमशो राजन्सर्वलोकाननुत्तमान् ॥२३॥
transliteration: duryodhanasahāyāśca rākṣasāḥ parikīrtitāḥ ,prāptāste kramaśo rājansarvalokānanuttamān.
english: O King, those rākṣasas, who were declared to be Duryodhana's allies, gradually attained all the supreme worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. O King, those rākṣasas, who were declared to be Duryodhana's allies, gradually attained all the supreme worlds.

sanskrit: भवनं च महेन्द्रस्य कुबेरस्य च धीमतः ।वरुणस्य तथा लोकान्विविशुः पुरुषर्षभाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: bhavanaṁ ca mahendrasya kuberasya ca dhīmataḥ ,varuṇasya tathā lokānviviśuḥ puruṣarṣabhāḥ.
english: And those foremost among men entered the abode of Mahendra, and of the intelligent Kubera, and similarly the realms of Varuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 24. And those foremost among men entered the abode of Mahendra, and of the intelligent Kubera, and similarly the realms of Varuna.

sanskrit: एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं विस्तरेण महाद्युते ।कुरूणां चरितं कृत्स्नं पाण्डवानां च भारत ॥२५॥
transliteration: etatte sarvamākhyātaṁ vistareṇa mahādyute ,kurūṇāṁ caritaṁ kṛtsnaṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca bhārata.
english: O greatly resplendent one, O descendant of Bharata, I have narrated to you in detail this entire account of the deeds of the Kurus and the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 25. O greatly resplendent one, O descendant of Bharata, I have narrated to you in detail this entire account of the deeds of the Kurus and the Pandavas.

sanskrit: सूत उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा द्विजश्रेष्ठात्स राजा जनमेजयः ।विस्मितोऽभवदत्यर्थं यज्ञकर्मान्तरेष्वथ ॥२६॥
transliteration: sūta uvāca ,etacchrutvā dvijaśreṣṭhātsa rājā janamejayaḥ ,vismito'bhavadatyarthaṁ yajñakarmāntareṣvatha.
english: Sūta said: Then, having heard this from the best of the twice-born (dvijaśreṣṭha), King Janamejaya became exceedingly astonished during the intervals of the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 26. Sūta said: Then, having heard this from the best of the twice-born (dvijaśreṣṭha), King Janamejaya became exceedingly astonished during the intervals of the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: ततः समापयामासुः कर्म तत्तस्य याजकाः ।आस्तीकश्चाभवत्प्रीतः परिमोक्ष्य भुजंगमान् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataḥ samāpayāmāsuḥ karma tattasya yājakāḥ ,āstīkaścābhavatprītaḥ parimokṣya bhujaṁgamān.
english: Then, his priests completed that rite. And Āstīka became pleased, having released the serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 27. Then, his priests completed that rite. And Āstīka became pleased, having released the serpents.

sanskrit: ततो द्विजातीन्सर्वांस्तान्दक्षिणाभिरतोषयत् ।पूजिताश्चापि ते राज्ञा ततो जग्मुर्यथागतम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato dvijātīnsarvāṁstāndakṣiṇābhiratoṣayat ,pūjitāścāpi te rājñā tato jagmuryathāgatam.
english: Then, he pleased all those twice-born with gifts (dakṣiṇā). Having been honored by the king, they then departed the way they had come.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 28. Then, he pleased all those twice-born with gifts (dakṣiṇā). Having been honored by the king, they then departed the way they had come.

sanskrit: विसर्जयित्वा विप्रांस्तान्राजापि जनमेजयः ।ततस्तक्षशिलायाः स पुनरायाद्गजाह्वयम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: visarjayitvā viprāṁstānrājāpi janamejayaḥ ,tatastakṣaśilāyāḥ sa punarāyādgajāhvayam.
english: Having dismissed those brahmins, King Janamejaya then returned from Takṣaśilā to Hastināpura.
search_text: Mahabharata Svargārohaṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 29. Having dismissed those brahmins, King Janamejaya then returned from Takṣaśilā to Hastināpura.

sanskrit: एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं वैशंपायनकीर्तितम् ।व्यासाज्ञया समाख्यातं सर्पसत्रे नृपस्य ह ॥३०॥
transliteration: etatte sarvamākhyātaṁ vaiśaṁpāyanakīrtitam ,vyāsājñayā samākhyātaṁ sarpasatre nṛpasya ha.
english: All this has been narrated to you, as proclaimed by Vaiśampāyana. Indeed, it was completely recounted by the command of (Vyāsa) during the king's serpent (Vedic ritual).
